title: Par Lankin
content:

{{Quote|Lankin is a cunning warrior and a dangerous foe. I personally conducted several operations in his sector, and have found his soldiers to be brave and of superior capabilities. They are driven, as if by a madman.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="W" />}}
'''Par Lankin''' was the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]]. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], he became one of the many Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]], keeping his strategic area under control. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] did not launch any offensive against Lankin, even when [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] of [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] filled reports on the threat posed by such a man. Intelligence reports mentioned that Lankin and his subordinate, [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Serdif Tount]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rintonne/Legends|Rintonne]], had attacked several worlds that the New Republic had recently acquired, conquering at least three of them. By [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the Lambda sector was no longer Lankin's personal kingdom.

Although Lankin never proved to be a formidable opponent to the New Republic, he was a good tactician and an expert commander. General Airen Cracken respected his abilities to the point of offering a sizable [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for his head. Moff Lankin kept most of his personal activities in secret, but he was known to enjoy hunting on [[Xandil VII]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|While New Republic High Command seems to have decided not to take preventative measures against this man, I can assure you that conquest is his foremost plan.|General Airen Cracken, on Par Lankin|<ref name="W" />}}
Par Lankin, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was born in [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Desstious]],<ref name="W" /> in the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> As an adult, Lankin became an [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. As [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], Lankin fought in the [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]] against the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] during the [[Noolian Crisis]].<ref name="TEGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> Later Lankin become the [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], or [[Sector/Legends|sectorial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]], of Lambda sector.<ref name="W" /> The sector had strategic importance for the Empire, as it had several heavy industry planets and [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]]—all of them under Moff Lankin's direct control.<ref name="W2">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] datapage 23</ref>

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Lankin and other several high-ranking Imperial officers tried to take control over their domains for themselves, instead of as representatives of the Emperor. These officers were known as [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] and, during the first several years, devoted their time to stock-pile military assets for future confrontations. A report by [[Dirk Harkness]] to [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Intelligence|Intelligence]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] only six months after Endor already listed Moff Lankin as one of the warlords building up their independent empires.<ref name="J">{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant}}</ref> A later report by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Baden Lathe]] of the [[Analysis Bureau (New Republic Intelligence)|Analysis Bureau]] specified that warlords such as Lankin or [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gaen Drommel]] had charge of relatively few resources when compared to other more successful warlords such as [[Zsinj]] and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ardus Kaine]].<ref name="T">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' datapage 3</ref>

Once the military of the New Republic had dealt with the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] in 4 ABY, they aimed to reach the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] without unifying the warlords. The Republic Admirals decided to isolate the weaker warlords in their own territories. Once warlords [[Utoxx Prentioch]] and [[Sander Delvardus|Delvardus]] were cut off by the Republic fleet, and after securing a new headquarters on the planet [[Moorja/Legends|Moorja]], the Republic Admirals made one single movement to scrutiny Lankin and Lambda sector. Having done so, they continued their way toward [[Glom Tho]] and [[Kalarba/Legends|Kalarba]].<ref name="A">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]met with Moff [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]] at [[Hosk Station/Legends|Hosk Station]], and one of Panaka's first queries was whether Lankin had surrendered or joined the New Republic. Organa responded that Lankin had been confined to<ref name="Warfare">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-6-showdown-at-naboo|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 6: Showdown at Naboo}}</ref> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="W2" /> [[Rintonne/Legends|Rintonne]].<ref name="Warfare" />

Moff Lankin kept control over Lambda sector for three more [[Standard year/Legends|years]], with the New Republic not initiating any measure against him. Lankin provided his subordinate, Governor [[Serdif Tount]] of the Rintonne, with [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] and [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] troops to control the civilian population of his world. In turn, Governor Tount helped Lankin with a campaign of raids over several New Republic planets; this tandem succeeded in taking over three of these as of [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].<ref name="W2" /> In fact, Rintonne became a [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] for Lankin's lands.<ref name="A" /> Lankin was also known to use the services of [[Rodin Baem]], an Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] that worked for several warlords.<ref name="W3">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] datapage 77</ref>

General Cracken performed several military operations in Lambda and discovered that Lankin's soldiers had courage, honed skills and a high motivation that, Cracken hypothesized, could be caused by having a madman as their commander. Cracken also noticed that Imperial patrol [[Starship/Legends|starships]] had been spotted by the patrol [[Droid/Legends|droids]] watching over planets that the New Republic had recently conquered and that lacked fortifications. Cracken presented reports insisting that Lankin was measuring the response time of those worlds and that he prepared an attack over their weaker assets. Against Cracken's advice, the New Republic decided not to take preemptive measures against Lankin. Cracken nonetheless offered a high [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for Lankin's head.<ref name="W" />

By 7 ABY, Lambda sector was no longer Lankin's personal fiefdom, and Lankin never became a real threat for the stability of the New Republic.<ref name="A" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I can only believe that those encounters with his ships are meant to size up our defenses and response times. It is an indication that he is readying a strike against our weaker worlds.|General Airen Cracken, on Par Lankin|<ref name="W" />}}
Moff Lankin was short in stature, reaching only 1.60 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] of height. General Cracken considered him to be sly and a good [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of his Imperial troops. Lankin was also an excellent tactician, specialized in working with fleets and with [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. Lankin commonly carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] with him.<ref name="W" />

Lankin was a reserved person who commonly remained in [[Lankin's palace|his personal palace]]. However, Lankin enjoyed an occasional hunting expedition in the [[Equatorial Reserves]] of the planet [[Xandil VII]].<ref name="W" />

==Behind the scenes==
Moff Lankin first appeared in [[Bill Smith]]'s [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] guide [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] ([[1993]]), published by [[West End Games]]. The book included an image of Lankin drawn by artist [[Mike Vilardi]]. Lankin was presented as a non-player character intended to be used to oppose the efforts of the player characters,<ref name="W" /> and was mentioned in the entries of two other characters of the book, Serdif Tount<ref name="W2" /> and Rodin Baem.<ref name="W3" /> Lankin was mentioned in passing in two other roleplaying supplements, the book ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]'' ([[1997]])<ref name="T" /> and the article ''[[Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant]]'' published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 2|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' #2]] ([[1994]]).<ref name="J" /> His defeat was sketched in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]),<ref name="A" /> and was established to have played a role in the Galactic Empire's early conflicts in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ([[2012]]).<ref name="TEGTW" />
